content.fact: During Year 3 before Sirius's innocence becomes known to Harry's circle, the public believes Sirius Black is a dangerous fugitive.
content.injection: During Year 3, most people should treat Sirius Black as a dangerous fugitive; do not make his innocence public knowledge before our story reveals it. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Prisoner of Azkaban: Year 3; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: Keep public belief separate from the private truth about Sirius.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Prisoner of Azkaban: Year 3; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
This card should be promoted when its characters, topics, or objects appear because it prevents timeline leakage.
antiLore: Do not have ordinary students or the wider public speak as if Sirius is exonerated before the relevant reveal.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.
Do not reveal, assume, or discuss this outcome before the relevant anchor/window is active unless the user has created an AU branch.

content.fact: Harry, Ron, and Hermione know Sirius was not the traitor, but this is not public knowledge.
